SUMMARY:
Successful case studies demonstrate that additive manufacturing can be integrated without disrupting existing processes, making it a viable

FULL CONTENT:
## Main Navigation

# How additive manufacturing is shaping the future of medical devices

![](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/29/2024/07/AdobeStock_512547080-1920x1080-1.jpg)

The medical device industry is facing a range of challenges, from increased raw material prices to global supply chain disruptions. Additive manufacturing is emerging as a transformative approach, offering a way to tackle these issues while enabling the production of highly customized medical devices such as implants, prosthetics and surgical guides.

With the additive manufacturing market for medical devices projected to reach $15.35 billion by 2032 ([Precedence Research](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/healthcare-additive-manufacturing-market)), there is a clear trend toward increased adoption of this technology. Using Siemens’ end-to-end additive manufacturing solutions, companies are creating personalized medical devices tailored to individual patient needs. For example, **[Unlimited Tomorrow](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/case-study-unlimited-tomorrow?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing)**‘s personalized prosthetics and **[LimaCorporate](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/case-study-limacorporate?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing)**‘s innovative knee implants demonstrate the tangible benefits of additive manufacturing.

To get a quick overview of how additive manufacturing drives innovation, check out the infographic **[Achieve Operational Excellence with additive manufacturing for medical devices](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/infographic-design-and-produce-modern-medical-devices-with-additive-manufacturing/?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing)**.

## Customization at its best

Customization is one of the standout advantages of additive manufacturing. Generative design and computer-aided design (CAD) tools enable the creation of medical devices that are optimized for each patient’s unique anatomy. These tools allow for precision and customization that traditional manufacturing methods simply cannot achieve**.**  
Another significant advantage of additive manufacturing is its economic benefits. The ability to produce single units cost-effectively without the need for expensive molds and tooling offers long-term cost savings.

Check out the case study, [**NX helps revolutionize the remote development of personalized arm prosthetics for kids**](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/case-study-unlimited-tomorrow?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing).

![](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/29/2024/01/Unlimited-Tomorrow-Prosthetic-image-1024x643.jpg)

## Key benefits of additive manufacturing

Ensuring regulatory compliance and quality assurance is critical in the medical device industry. Siemens’ additive manufacturing solutions make this easier by using digital twins and centralized production information to maintain traceability and high-quality standards. This ensures that the final products meet all regulatory requirements, giving manufacturers peace of mind.

Efficiency and automation are also key benefits of additive manufacturing. Automated planning and scheduling enhance production efficiency and consistency, reducing production time while ensuring high-quality outputs. This is particularly important in a field where time-to-market can be a critical factor in success. Integration with existing manufacturing systems is a concern for many, but [Siemens’ solutions](https://www.sw.siemens.com/en-US/digital-thread/smart-manufacturing/medical-device-manufacturing/?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing) are designed for seamless adoption. Successful case studies demonstrate that additive manufacturing can be integrated without disrupting existing processes, making it a viable option for companies looking to innovate without overhauling their entire production setup.

**[LimaCorporate](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/case-study-limacorporate?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing)**‘s use of Siemens software to create implants with advanced lattice structures is a prime example of how additive manufacturing is pushing the boundaries of what is possible.

![Man holding a knee implant designed with additive manufacturing](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/29/2024/01/Additive-Manufacturing-for-MD-1920x1080-1-1024x576.jpg)

Read the case study **[Leveraging additive manufacturing solution to reduce modeling and job preparation time by 50 percent](https://resources.sw.siemens.com/en-US/case-study-limacorporate?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing).**

## **The future of additive manufacturing in the medical device industry**

As we look ahead, the future potential of additive manufacturing in the medical device industry is promising. Supported by digitalization and advanced technologies like AI and machine learning, additive manufacturing offers continuous improvement and innovation in manufacturing processes.

The ongoing transformation in medical device manufacturing is empowering both companies and patients. Discover how Siemens’ [Operational Excellence](https://www.sw.siemens.com/en-US/digital-thread/smart-manufacturing/medical-device-manufacturing/?ref=blog-infographic-additive-manufacturing) tools can help you master today’s additive manufacturing challenges.

![](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/c99f1199b58695dd6ae4fc487c7475a3?s=480&r=pg&d=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.auth0.com%2Favatars%2Fan.png)

AnnaMary Gualdoni is a senior digital content marketing specialist for the in the medical device and pharmaceutical industries at Siemens Digital Industries Software. She creates content for medical device designers and manufacturers to create safe, compliant products that prioritize patient well-being and help industry professionals overcome the unique challenges of MedTech and bring life-changing devices to market.

[More from this author](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/medical-devices-pharmaceuticals/author/annamary-gualdoni/ "Read more from this author")

--- SOURCE 2: Case Study: Additive Manufacturing for Medical Applications ---
URL: https://apiumtec.com/en/case-study-additive-manufacturing-for-medical-applications

SUMMARY:
In the following case study, it is shown that 3D printing PEEK for medical application is not only material efficient but also reduces time and cost.

FULL CONTENT:
![](data:image/png;base64,)
![Apium 3D Technologies GmbH](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/Apium_Transparent_Without_ATGBMH.png)

# Additive Manufacturing for Medical Applications

## Produce patient-specific medical implants, which are affordable and accessible for everyone in the world.

![](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/skull_freigestellt.png)

Part Design: Dr. Philipp Honigmann, Dr. Florian Thieringer  
OSPIX: Sanjay Pathak, Global Health Care

### **Cost and Time Efficient Manufacture of Implants**

**Additive manufacturing is a cost and time saving process for single parts and small****series production.** By using the material extrusion process technology, the Apium M220 makes it now possible to manufacture implants and prosthetics. The thermoplastic PEEK (polyether-ether-ketone) has established its position as a suitable material for long-term implants and it is ideal for the material extrusion process in order to manufacture mechanically resilient parts.

Conventionally, PEEK implants are milled which produces material waste by nature. **In the following case study, it is shown that 3D printing PEEK for medical application is not only material efficient but also reduces time and cost.**

![](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Medical_Process_Integration.png)

#### **Design, Slice and Print with M220**

Our 3D printing technology can be easily integrated into an existing medical workflow. Data used to reconstruct defects are converted into a machine code that results in component generation by the M220.

#### **Manufacturing Advantages with Material Extrusion Technology**

Using the example of a patient-specific skull implant, the time and cost savings compared to a conventionally manufactured implant are demonstrated.

First of all, the skull implant was loaded with 2.99 kN on a small area before the component cracked. A human skull withstands a force of 2.88 kN in a comparable test [1].

*[1] C. Van Lierde, B. Depreitere, J. Vander Sloten, R. Van Audekercke, G. Van Der Perre und J. Goffin,**„Skull biomechanics: The energy absorbability of the human skull frontal bone during fracture under quasi**static loading,“ Journal of Applied Biomaterials & Biomechanics, 2003.*

![](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Static_Test_1.jpg)
![](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Static_Test_2.jpg)
![](https://apiumtec.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/M220_side.jpg)

#### **Apium M220 3D Printing Technology vs Milling Technology**

Comparing the manufacturing cost and time for the same skull implant produced by milling and 3D printing, the results of this case study show that 3D printing technology provides:

Additionally, parts produced with an Apium M220 have the following benefits:

--- SOURCE 3: Additive manufacturing for medical and healthcare - Renishaw ---
URL: https://www.renishaw.com/en/additive-manufacturing-for-medical-and-healthcare--44309?srsltid=AfmBOooaYEUZ7rYhcr4q45ZHJfTouR-IweW8IxKsWl4sPoS5yAARLVLE

SUMMARY:
The latest medical and healthcare additive manufacturing case studies from Renishaw.

FULL CONTENT:
# Additive manufacturing for medical and healthcare

Latest additive manufacturing case studies.

## 

## Case studies

### [Renishaw goes big on nanofiber](/en/renishaw-goes-big-on-nanofiber--45866 "Renishaw goes big on nanofiber")

To help with ongoing personal protective equipment (PPE) shortages, Ontario-based nanofiber manufacturer, BIG-nano, has collaborated with Renishaw to create a local supply chain for protective masks using additive manufacturing (AM).

![Renishaw goes big on nanofiber](/media/thumbnails/180high/f245b23934e345a5b4168ae35897c9d8.jpg)
![Renishaw goes big on nanofiber](/media/thumbnails/100/f245b23934e345a5b4168ae35897c9d8.jpg)

### [Renishaw goes big on nanofiber](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/renishaw-goes-big-on-nanofiber--118875 "Renishaw goes big on nanofiber")

To help with ongoing personal protective equipment (PPE) shortages, Ontario-based nanofiber manufacturer, BIG-nano, has collaborated with Renishaw to create a local supply chain for protective masks using additive manufacturing (AM). BIG-nano developed a fine liquid blowing technique for the production of nanofiber, allowing it to more easily scale production at affordable costs. Renishaw used one of its RenAM 500Q system located in Canada to build a novel titanium nozzle for the liquid blowing process, capable of producing N99 quality fabric, while reducing maintenance costs and downtime.

### [Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants](/en/additive-manufacturing-for-serial-production-of-orthopaedic-implants--45351 "Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants")

Croom Precision Medical has chosen Renishaw as their additive manufacturing (AM) and metrology partner, utilising Renishaw technology throughout the production and validation of their ISO13485 certified medical devices. The company has found that using Renishaw's AM technology allows them to incorporate complex features into their implant design at a commercially viable cost.

![Case study:  Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants](/media/thumbnails/180high/a28bb8ddbc2d41559333bd4b7a38d473.jpg)
![Case study:  Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants](/media/thumbnails/100/a28bb8ddbc2d41559333bd4b7a38d473.jpg)

### [Case study: Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-additive-manufacturing-for-serial-production-of-orthopaedic-implants--116037 "Case study: Additive manufacturing for serial production of orthopaedic implants")

Croom Precision Medical has chosen Renishaw as their additive manufacturing (AM) and metrology partner, utilising Renishaw technology throughout the production and validation of their ISO13485 certificed medical devices. The company has found that using Renishaw’s AM technology allows them to incorporate complex features into their implant design at a commercially viable cost. In a heavily regulated environment, Renishaw’s built-in AM software and metrology solutions allows CPM to maintain traceability throughout their manufacturing process.

### [Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants](/en/streamlining-additive-manufacturing-for-spinal-implants--44281 "Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants")

Global engineering company, Renishaw, collaborated with two innovative technology companies to demonstrate the capability of metal additive manufacturing (AM) technology to produce lightweight spinal implants that mimic the mechanical properties of bone. As part of the project, Irish Manufacturing Research (IMR) designed and manufactured a series of representative spinal implants. IMR designed the implants using software from nTopology and produced them on a Renishaw RenAM 500M metal AM system.

![Case study:  Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants](/media/thumbnails/180high/03f7758b36f34044a46029fc29e33d61.jpg)
![Case study:  Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants](/media/thumbnails/100/03f7758b36f34044a46029fc29e33d61.jpg)

### [Case study: Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-streamlining-additive-manufacturing-for-spinal-implants--107177 "Case study: Streamlining additive manufacturing for spinal implants")

Global engineering company, Renishaw, collaborated with two innovative technology companies to demonstrate the capability of metal additive manufacturing (AM) technology to produce lightweight spinal implants that mimic the mechanical properties of bone. As part of the project, Irish Manufacturing Research (IMR) designed and manufactured a series of representative spinal implants. IMR designed the implants using software from nTopology and produced them on a Renishaw RenAM 500M metal AM system.

### [A swift transition to digital dentistry](/en/--40920 "A Swift transition to digital dentistry")

A partnership with Renishaw saw the introduction of additive manufacturing to Swift, fitting seamlessly into a newly implemented digital workflow. Renishaw supplied an AM250 system, which uses a high-powered ytterbium fibre laser to fuse fine metallic powders together to create a final three-dimensional structure.

![Case study:  A Swift transition to digital dentistry](/media/thumbnails/180high/d02294492f4748e7a1523c99a262ca45.jpg)
![Case study:  A Swift transition to digital dentistry](/media/thumbnails/100/d02294492f4748e7a1523c99a262ca45.jpg)

### [Case study: A Swift transition to digital dentistry](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-a-swift-transition-to-digital-dentistry--90052 "Case study: A Swift transition to digital dentistry")

The process of lost-wax casting is still used today and hasn't changed much since ancient Greece. Commonly used in jewellery making, it is also a common manufacturing method in dentistry. Albeit a time-honoured process, Swift Dental Group recently sourced a more accurate and efficient manufacturing method to use at its facility in Bolton, UK with the help of global engineering and scientific technologies company, Renishaw.

### [AM in veterinary surgery – saving a well-loved member of the family](/en/additive-manufacturing-in-veterinary-surgery-saving-a-well-loved-member-of-the-family--42950)

A seven-year-old Bernese Mountain Dog with a tumour on the left side of his maxilla (upper jaw) had few options other than total excision of the growth followed by reconstruction. A customised 3D printed titanium implant supporting the dog's bone structure was the most appropriate treatment due to the complexity of the region, requiring significant design and manufacturing freedom.

![Case study:  Additive manufacturing in veterinary surgery - saving a well-loved member of the family](/media/thumbnails/180high/578974681a7a43458d48d44ff1cf1d5d.jpg)
![Case study:  Additive manufacturing in veterinary surgery - saving a well-loved member of the family](/media/thumbnails/100/578974681a7a43458d48d44ff1cf1d5d.jpg)

### [Case study: Additive manufacturing in veterinary surgery - saving a well-loved member of the family](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-additive-manufacturing-in-veterinary-surgery-saving-a-well-loved-member-of-the-family--98590 "Case study: Additive manufacturing in veterinary surgery - saving a well-loved member of the family")

A truly international effort, involving input from Germany, Canada and the UK has seen additive manufacturing (AM), also known as metal 3D printing, used to great effect to help save a Canadian family's dog.
In a recent procedure, Renishaw helped to produce an implant designed by Voxelmed in Germany that was fitted in Canada, to replace the hard tissue lost due to tumour removal.

### [Proslab ensures absolute accuracy with AM](/en/proslab-ensures-absolute-accuracy-with-additive-manufacturing--43311)

Proslab turned to global engineering company Renishaw to fully digitalise its manufacturing process using additive manufacturing.Before working with Renishaw, Proslab introduced a partially digital workflow, using 3D scanning and design tools to design dentures.

![Case study:  Proslab ensures absolute accuracy with additive manufacturing](/media/thumbnails/180high/05634daf137e4da4893f91bdd5bed1c2.jpg)
![Case study:  Proslab ensures absolute accuracy with additive manufacturing](/media/thumbnails/100/05634daf137e4da4893f91bdd5bed1c2.jpg)

### [Case study: Proslab ensures absolute accuracy with additive manufacturing](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-proslab-ensures-absolute-accuracy-with-additive-manufacturing--100871 "Case study: Proslab ensures absolute accuracy with additive manufacturing")

Leading Australian dental laboratory, Proslab, has fully digitalised the manufacture of its cobalt chrome removable partial dentures (RPDs). The laboratory partnered with global engineering company, Renishaw, to install what is thought to be the first metal additive manufacturing machine for dental
applications in Australia.

### [3D modelling and printing](/en/3d-modelling-and-printing-saving-theatre-time-and-providing-excellent-patient-outcome--42114)

Complex reconstructive surgery can significantly benefit from a digital workflow and pre-planning processes to help optimise operating theatre productivity with excellent surgical results. Read how Renishaw used its additive manufacturing (metal 3D printing) expertise to help the University Hospital of Wales (UHW) treat a patient who suffered cancer of the lower jaw and required removal of the affected region coupled with reconstructive work.

![Case study:  3D modelling and printing - saving theatre time and providing excellent patient outcome](/media/thumbnails/180high/4ba997ffb68c44a39186e6b990adc703.jpg)
![Case study:  3D modelling and printing - saving theatre time and providing excellent patient outcome](/media/thumbnails/100/4ba997ffb68c44a39186e6b990adc703.jpg)

### [Case study: 3D modelling and printing - saving theatre time and providing excellent patient outcome](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-3d-modelling-and-printing-saving-theatre-time-and-providing-excellent-patient-outcome--87951 "Case study: 3D modelling and printing - saving theatre time and providing excellent patient outcome")

Complex reconstructive surgery can significantly benefit from a digital workflow and pre-planning processes to help optimise operating theatre productivity with excellent surgical results. Read how Renishaw used its additive manufacturing (metal 3D printing) expertise to help the University Hospital of Wales (UHW) treat a patient who suffered cancer of the lower jaw and required removal of the affected region coupled with reconstructive work.

### [Mission to Nepal](/en/mission-to-nepal-surgical-education-and-innovation-in-disaster-affected-regions--39918)

Metal 3D printing's real life applications are ever increasing. Read how a NRIEF's mission to Nepal has taken advantage of this technology by helping a young mother who was a road traffic collision victim.

![Case study:  Mission to Nepal - surgical education and innovation in remote regions](/media/thumbnails/180high/6379e326e0354e8ca04e6ecd94cdb045.jpg)
![Case study:  Mission to Nepal - surgical education and innovation in remote regions](/media/thumbnails/100/6379e326e0354e8ca04e6ecd94cdb045.jpg)

### [Case study: Mission to Nepal - surgical education and innovation in remote regions](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-mission-to-nepal-surgical-education-and-innovation-in-remote-regions--88014 "Case study: Mission to Nepal - surgical education and innovation in remote regions")

Metal 3D printing's real life applications are ever increasing. Read how a NRIEF's mission to Nepal has taken advantage of this technology by helping a young mother who was a road traffic collision victim.

### [Digital evolution of cranial surgery](/en/digital-evolution-of-cranial-surgery--38602)

From complex reconstructive facial surgery to orthopaedic and trauma surgery, advances in additive manufacturing have inspired a growing number of progressive surgeons to commission metal 3D printed patient specific implants (PSIs) and cutting guides for both complex and straightforward procedures.

![Case study:  Digital evolution of cranial surgery](/media/thumbnails/180high/325077bc6c5144139ada8bd74340e7f3.jpg)
![Case study:  Digital evolution of cranial surgery](/media/thumbnails/100/325077bc6c5144139ada8bd74340e7f3.jpg)

### [Case study: Digital evolution of cranial surgery](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-digital-evolution-of-cranial-surgery--91739 "Case study: Digital evolution of cranial surgery")

From complex reconstructive facial surgery to orthopaedic and trauma surgery, advances in additive manufacturing have inspired a growing number of progressive surgeons to commission metal 3D printed patient specific implants (PSIs) and cutting guides for both complex and straightforward procedures.

### [Egan adopts digital workflow for Removable Partial Dentures (RPD)](/en/egan-adopts-digital-workflow-for-removable-partial-dentures-rpd--44401)

Global engineering company Renishaw worked with Egan Dental Laboratory to fully digitise the design and manufactureof Removable Partial Dentures (RPDs), also known as chromes. The move to digital techniques halved the time required to make a chrome framework and resulted in the production of stronger, more accurate, yet thinner RPDs.

![Case study:  Egan adopts digital workflow for Removable Partial Dentures (RPD)](/media/thumbnails/180high/df7d3f5de7dd4c21abc0342365c64dfe.jpg)
![Case study:  Egan adopts digital workflow for Removable Partial Dentures (RPD)](/media/thumbnails/100/df7d3f5de7dd4c21abc0342365c64dfe.jpg)

### [Case study: Egan adopts digital workflow for Removable Partial Dentures (RPD)](https://www.renishaw.com/resourcecentre/en/download/case-study-egan-adopts-digital-workflow-for-removable-partial-dentures-rpd--107818 "Case study: Egan adopts digital workflow for Removable Partial Dentures (RPD)")

Global engineering company Renishaw worked with Egan Dental Laboratory to fully digitise the design and manufacture of Removable Partial Dentures (RPDs), also known as chromes. The move to digital techniques halved the time required to make a chrome framework and resulted in the production of stronger, more accurate, yet thinner RPDs.

# Case Studies and Guides

## Making Innovation Possible

Learn how companies use our digital manufacturing services to solve complex challenges and bring their concepts to market faster and more cost-effectively, time and time again.

## Additive Manufacturing Case Studies and Guides

In our additive manufacturing case studies and guides, learn how our [3D printing services](https://tth-3994606.hs-sites.com/services/3d-printing-service?hsLang=en&__hstc=173546300.f7b864b03ec4b8e108c91a6f91e1da08.1726692143599.1728043045146.1728047009673.8&__hssc=173546300.4.1728047009673&__hsfp=782131645) are able to solve some of our customers' biggest challenges.

![Cyclist operating a handcycle](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Picture5.jpeg?width=539&height=359&name=Picture5.jpeg)

### Custom 3D Printing Solution Reliably Delivers High-Quality Product for Less

Learn the benefits of partnering with The Technology House (TTH) when you need innovative, high-quality 3D-printed products on time and under budget.

![PostPro3D](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/PostPro3D.jpg?width=2000&name=PostPro3D.jpg)

### How The Technology House's Unique Vapor Smoothing Process Adds Value to 3D Printing

Learn the advantages of partnering with The Technology House for vapor smoothing and the dramatic results it can produce.

![Cleveland Landmark](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Case-Study-Concept-to-Production.jpg?width=470&height=530&name=Case-Study-Concept-to-Production.jpg)

### From Concept to Production in Two Weeks

A company needed to design and manufacture 3D-printed ornaments and magnets that featured local Cleveland landmarks. Learn how TTH used Color Jet Printing to create parts with the appropriate texture and colors, truly giving these products a hometown feel.

![Skull implant model](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Case-Study-Reducing-Surgical-Time.jpg?width=470&height=530&name=Case-Study-Reducing-Surgical-Time.jpg)

### Reducing Surgical Time

See how one of our customers uses models to ensure their implants will fit patients properly before surgery. Using the stereolithography process, we create models of both the patient’s skull and the implant from the customer-provided CAT scan data. In this case study on stereolithography, learn how we've reduced the amount of time needed to create the implant as well as reduced surgery time by eight to twelve hours.

![Vitamix Innovative Nozzle](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Vitamix-Case-Study/Image-1.jpg?width=470&height=530&name=Image-1.jpg)

### Vitamix Produces 10x More Durable Parts at Scale with TTH using Carbon DLS Technology

Read this case study to learn how Vitamix collaborated with TTH and Carbon to completely redesign and produce an innovative nozzle that is 10x more durable and 30% more economical than the previous nozzle. Vitamix is in the process of deploying tens of thousands of these nozzles into stores.

![Seeker robotic free flier inspector](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/NASA-Case-Study/Image-1.jpg?width=470&height=311&name=Image-1.jpg)

### NASA Seeker Robot x TTH Powered by Carbon

Learn how Carbon additive technology allowed the NASA team to successfully pursue agile development for space hardware and how the Carbon Platform + plastic material Cyanate Ester 221 was validated as a viable production solution for their nimble venture into new robotics.

![Material Comparison Chart](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Material-Comparison-Chart-thumbnail.png?width=800&height=594&name=Material-Comparison-Chart-thumbnail.png)

### Additive Material Comparison Chart

There are many factors to consider when selecting additive materials to ensure your project meets all industry standards and requirements. Our additive comparison chart will help you select the right materials.

![The Ultimate Guide to Polymer 3D Printing Handbook](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/thumbnail-TTH-3D-Handbook-V2_1.png?width=800&height=752&name=thumbnail-TTH-3D-Handbook-V2_1.png)

### The Ultimate Guide to Polymer 3D Printing

With vast improvements in technology, materials, and part finishes, 3D-printed parts are not only strong enough to compete with the likes of machined and injection-molded parts, but they can look the part too.

## Urethane Casting Case Studies and Guides

Learn how we use the urethane casting process to find manufacturing solutions to our customer’s biggest challenges.

![Cast urethane part](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Case-Study-Urethane-Casting-makes-sense-for-Production.jpg?width=470&height=311&name=Case-Study-Urethane-Casting-makes-sense-for-Production.jpg)

### Urethane Casting Makes Sense for Production

A lower upfront investment on tooling, ease of making changes, and fast turnaround—these are just some of the benefits of choosing cast urethane for production parts. Learn more about why this TTH customer chose this process over injection molding.

## Injection Molding Case Studies and Guides

Learn how we use the different injection molding solutions to fit our customer’s cost, quality and timing needs.

![Injection mold production equipment](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Case-Study-Tool-Costs.jpg?width=470&height=530&name=Case-Study-Tool-Costs.jpg)

### Save on Tooling Costs and Improve Manufacturability with TTH

A medical device company came to TTH to move a project from prototype to injection mold production. The problem was that the part’s original design appearance could not be altered, which would have made it very costly and difficult to injection mold. Learn how we were both able to save time and money by establishing a relationship early on in the process.

![The Ultimate Guide to Injection Molding](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/TTH-Injection-Mo.png?width=613&height=785&name=TTH-Injection-Mo.png)

### The Ultimate Guide to Injection Molding

When businesses need high volumes of intricate parts, they turn to plastic injection molding. This process provides a high level of production while minimizing the cost per unit.

## Machining Case Studies and Guides

Learn how we adapt to new challenges with specialized equipment to meet the requirements of our customers.

![Aerospace air foils produces by electrical discharge machining](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Website/Website%20Images/Case%20Studies/Case-Study-Moving-Beyond-Prototyping-to-Production.jpg?width=470&height=311&name=Case-Study-Moving-Beyond-Prototyping-to-Production.jpg)

### Moving Beyond Prototyping to Production

A local aerospace manufacturer needed to remove gates (the leftover material from the channel used to fill a mold) from their airfoils using electrical discharge machining. Learn about their partnership with The Technology House (TTH) and how we are able to turn parts around within 48 hours.

![TTH CNC Machining Handbook ](https://www.tth.com/hs-fs/hubfs/thumbnail-TTH-CNC-Machining-Handbook_2.png?width=598&height=562&name=thumbnail-TTH-CNC-Machining-Handbook_2.png)

### CNC Machining Handbook

Learn best practices, industry standards, and how to choose the right partner for consistent quality and efficiency. Download TTH's CNC Machining Handbook now.

## Design Without Restriction

See how we partnered with Vitamix and Carbon to redefine what's possible. We took a legacy, six-piece, injection-molded part design and turned it into a new, one-piece, 3D-printed part.

## We'd Love to Hear from You

Our experts have a proven history of providing innovative solutions at every stage of product development—from product design through production.

![Let's Collaborate](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/3994606/aadb1812-abba-4091-94ec-c3e43f250957.png)

## Get Your Product to Market

Our integrated approach to design, prototyping, and production allows you to bring your concept to market faster and more cost-effectively than virtually anyone else.

### Bring Your Idea to Life

enriched_points.expanded_content: Additive manufacturing enables the creation of customized implants and prosthetics tailored to individual patient anatomies, improving fit, comfort, and efficacy in medical treatments.
The medical device industry faces significant regulatory hurdles for additive manufacturing, necessitating compliance with stringent guidelines to ensure safety and effectiveness of products.
Successful case studies, such as those by Unlimited Tomorrow and LimaCorporate, showcase how additive manufacturing has revolutionized personalized medical devices, enhancing patient outcomes.
Material standards are crucial in additive manufacturing for medical devices, ensuring that materials used meet safety, performance, and biocompatibility requirements for long-term implants.
The integration of additive manufacturing in healthcare holds immense potential, promising advancements in personalized medicine, reduced production costs, and improved patient care solutions.
